codeReview.summary: Pass. Commit `a95baaf8d2b7ff01d256b9e654dcc0244632ec9a` fixes the earlier route-suite weakening by running the full Express route stack rather than only the terminal handler in the rewritten auth-heavy tests. The new helpers in `server/src/__tests__/activity-routes.test.ts:23-100`, `server/src/__tests__/company-branding-route.test.ts:57-137`, and `server/src/__tests__/openclaw-invite-prompt-route.test.ts:74-171` rebuild each router, collect every handler from `route.stack`, and execute them sequentially, so these suites still exercise the real route-level validation and auth wiring for `GET /issues/:id/runs` and `GET /heartbeat-runs/:runId/issues` in `server/src/routes/activity.ts:77-98`, `PATCH /:companyId/branding` in `server/src/routes/companies.ts:295-316`, and the OpenClaw invite prompt route guarded by `validate(createOpenClawInvitePromptSchema)` in `server/src/routes/access.ts:2002-2038`. The routines stabilization is also appropriately narrow: `server/src/__tests__/routines-e2e.test.ts:31-33,79-91` switches to static imports for the real route/service factories while preserving the embedded-Postgres integration path and documented cleanup ordering at `server/src/__tests__/routines-e2e.test.ts:57-70`. I did not find a new high-confidence correctness risk in these flake fixes.

summary: Reviewed the feature metadata, handoff, transcript skeleton, backend-worker skill, mission shared-state docs, and commit `a95baaf8d2b7ff01d256b9e654dcc0244632ec9a`. The patch keeps the targeted server auth and validation suites deterministic without weakening their real route-stack coverage, and I did not find a new high-confidence correctness issue, so this review passes.
